title: santalum insulare var. alticola
intro: Santalum insulare var. alticola, also known as the Lord Howe Island sandalwood, is a variety of the Santalum insulare plant, a species of flowering plant in the sandalwood family. This plant is valued for its fragrant wood and is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Santalum insulare var. alticola are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, extracts, and infusions that can be used in aromatherapy, skincare products, and traditional remedies.
